publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-11-16 23:32:10 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Typescript report, letters, newspaper clippings, correspondence, memorandums, invitations, mayoral candidate promotional material, business, and seasonal cards, a booklet, and a scrapbook album of newspaper clippings of the New York politician and advocate of Prohibition, Palmer Canfield, document primarily Canfield's final Prohibition post (from 1929) as Federal Prohibition Director for Albany District, as well as his mayoral candidate campaign (1913-1921).

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Palmer Canfield papers primarily document Canfield's final Prohibition post (from 1929) as "Dry Chief" for the "wet" leaning Albany District and material from his political and legal career, including his mayoral campaigns in Kingston (1913-1921), New York, dating from 1913 to 1931. The collection contains a typescript report and letters, newspaper clippings, correspondence, memorandums, invitations, mayoral candidate promotional material, business, and seasonal cards, a booklet, and a scrapbook album of newspaper clippings.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Palmer Canfield was a New York politician and advocate of Prohibition, first as Special Assistant U.S. Attorney (1921) in the Southern District of New York and Legal Advisor at the Prohibition Department (1922) and later as Federal Prohibition Director for the State of New York (1923). Canfield had been an actively practicing attorney since 1904. He was also elected Mayor of Kingston, NY, from 1913 to 1921 on the "Honesty, Economy, Efficiency" platform. One of his main activities during his tenure as Mayor and before the National Prohibition, was the reduction of Kingston's salons to one-third of their original number (from 125 to 50). He was also one of the organizers of the Mayors Bureau of State, a Grand Chancellor of Knights of Pythia (1920-1921), and a member of the State and National Councils, Y.M.C.A., the executive committee N.Y. State Methodist Laymen's Association, the New York State Waterways Association, and more. He was married, and he had five children.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Palmer Canfield papers, #8771. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Bars (Drinking establishments)
Drinking of alcoholic beverages -- United States.
Liquor laws -- New York (State)
Prohibition.
Prohibition -- Law and legislation
Temperance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provocative letter, titled " A Valentine for the Dry Force and their Commissioner Old Mother Hubbard, who is such a dry old maid his bones rattle," possibly by a group of people, signed as the Repealers of Prohibition. The sender(s) encourage Canfield to read Times Union's letters to the editor about Prohibition Law repeal, including the actual newspaper clippings.

did.unittitle: Canfield Plans Survey for New Drive on Rum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clipping, with subtitle "Farm relief first on Legislative Slate; Times Union, The Sunday Telegram. Albany NY".

did.unittitle: Canfield's Unpublished Letter to Kingston Daily Freeman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer with an unpublished letter sent from Canfield as a candidate for Mayor to the Kingston Daily Freeman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nursery invoices; invitations; envelopes from Canfield's correspondence as the Federal Prohibition Director; election campaign cards for Palmer's candidacy as Mayor; Plamer's business and seasonal cards; booklet with the title "Manual for New York State Law Enforcement Officials and Local A.B.C. Boards. Program from the 15th Annual Reunion Dinner of the Ulster Country Society in NYC".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speech of Palmer Canfield, Jr., from his mayoral campaign; election promotional flyers.

did.unittitle: Report on Federal Prohibition Activities 2nd Federal District, March 1-December 31, 1926

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript report. Includes memorandums and correspondence (1927, January 14 -18)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several typescript copies of Canfield's resume. A letter from Natalie F. Cough, attorney, NYACK, NY in 1928. Canfield's correspondence as Federal Prohibition Director with G.E. Warner and Charles D. Hilles, the vice-chairman at the Republican National Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dozens of newspaper clippings, loose and affixed to scrapbook pages, detail Canfield's activity as the new Prohibition Administrator in the Albany district in 1929.
